description_long: This bulldozer features a robust, industrial design with a large front-mounted bucket loader, supported by two massive, deeply treaded tires on each side. The body is primarily painted in a muted yellow and black color scheme, with a sturdy, enclosed operator cabin that offers protection and visibility. The cabin is equipped with side mirrors and an antenna, suggesting modern communication and safety features. The rear of the vehicle is streamlined, with venting and detailing typical of heavy-duty construction equipment. The loader arm and hydraulic pistons are visible, indicating the machine's capability for lifting and moving heavy materials. The overall build is solid and functional, designed for demanding urban construction tasks.
description: A heavy-duty yellow and black bulldozer with a large front loader bucket, enclosed cabin, and rugged tires, built for urban construction work.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the bulldozer, the large front loader bucket, and the enclosed cabin with visible side mirrors and antenna.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back panel, rear lights, and the streamlined engine cover with venting details.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the loader arm, hydraulic pistons, and the cabin's protective structure.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the wide loader bucket, the front hydraulic system, and the symmetrical placement of the tires.
attribute_blade_type: Straight blade (S-blade); the bucket is flat and wide with no pronounced side wings or curvature, suitable for general-purpose loading.
attribute_track_type: Rubber tire system; the bulldozer uses four large, deeply treaded rubber tires instead of tracks, providing mobility on urban surfaces.
attribute_cabin_design: The cabin is fully enclosed with glass windows and features protective structures, including side mirrors and an antenna for operator safety and communication. The design prioritizes visibility and operator comfort.
attribute_exhaust_placement: Vertical stack on the right side of the cabin, extending upward.
